mnemonics.title: GREEN Transport Tips
mnemonics.content:
G - Green vehicles (EV, Hybrid)
R - Reduce/reuse trips
E - Electronic monitoring (ITS)
E - Encourage public transport
N - Non-motorized travel (walk/cycle)

mnemonics.title: Traffic Control Methods
mnemonics.content:
S - Signals (synchronized)
M - Monitoring (cameras/sensors)
A - Access control (LEZ)
R - Routing (GPS/navigation)
T - Teleworking (work from home)

mnemonics.title: Benefits of Sustainable Travel
mnemonics.content:
C - Cost savings
O - Obesity prevention (active travel)
R - Reduced congestion
T - Time savings (smoother flow)
R - Resource conservation
O - Opportunity for cleaner air
L - Lower emissions

acronyms.usage: Technology-based system for monitoring and managing traffic flow in real-time
Urban area with restrictions on high-polluting vehicles to improve air quality
Vehicle powered by electricity with zero tailpipe emissions
Gases like CO2 that trap heat in atmosphere, primarily from vehicle emissions
Primary greenhouse gas emitted from burning fossil fuels in vehicles
Harmful pollutants from vehicle engines contributing to smog and respiratory issues
Tiny particles from vehicle exhaust that penetrate deep into lungs
Camera system used for traffic monitoring and incident detection

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a factory chimney emitting thick black smoke - that's what idling and stop-start traffic does to our air, with each stuck vehicle adding to the pollution cloud above the city.
Picture a tree absorbing CO2 like a sponge - the more you drive, the more trees you'd need to plant to balance your carbon footprint.
Think of traffic signals as waves in the ocean - synchronized green lights let you 'surf' through intersections without stopping, saving energy like a surfer rides a wave.
Walking/cycling as 'human-powered vehicles' - like charging your own body's battery while reducing the load on the planet's resources.

analogies.analogy: Traffic signals are like orchestra conductors - when synchronized, all vehicles move smoothly in harmony; when out of sync, chaos ensues like a band playing randomly.
Carpooling is like a shared pizza - one pizza (car) feeds multiple people, saving money and reducing the number of pizzas (cars) ordered (on the road).
Intelligent Transportation System acts like a city's nervous system - sensors are nerve endings sending information to a central brain that controls traffic flow like brain signals to muscles.
Electric vehicles are like silent runners - they move efficiently without the heavy breathing (exhaust) of traditional runners, making less noise and leaving no pollution trails.
